author: Gasses
text:
Drop dead GIT_* scrub before the bl binary in tests

The integration-test helpers that launch `bl` — `tests/common::bl`,
`tests/common::bl_as`, and the `bl_fresh` closure in `tests/init.rs` —
each looped `GIT_ENV_VARS` and `env_remove`'d it from the
`assert_cmd::Command` before running the binary.

That scrub was inert. `bl` is already immune to an inherited `GIT_*`
environment: it discovers the repo via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`
and routes every git subprocess through `git::clean_git_command`, which
scrubs `GIT_ENV_VARS` on the production path. Scrubbing the env of an
already-immune binary buys nothing, and in practice `GIT_*` is not set
when the suite runs anyway. The loop read as if test isolation depended
on it when it did not.

Removing the three loops left the `pub use balls::git::GIT_ENV_VARS`
re-export in `tests/common/mod.rs` unused there — its only in-module
consumers were those loops. `tests/init.rs` is now the sole consumer
(three raw `git` StdCommand calls that genuinely bypass
`clean_git_command` and must scrub themselves), so the import moves to
init.rs directly rather than dangling as a dead re-export.

The `GIT_ENV_VARS` const in src/git.rs is unchanged.
